name_en: Japanese Cedar of Nakamura Kusushi Shrine
description_en: A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese cedar (sugi). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Nakamuramachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.

name_en: Japanese Cedar of Tanesato Hachimangū Shrine
description_en: A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese cedar (sugi). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Tanesatomachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.

name_en: Japanese Cedar and Japanese Red Pine of the Tanesato Mausoleum
description_en: A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese cedar (sugi) and a Japanese red pine (akamatsu). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Tanesatomachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.
A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a ginkgo (ichō). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Hideridamachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.
A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a ginkgo (ichō). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Tsurimachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.
A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese honey locust (saikachi). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Hitotsumorimachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.
A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese yew (ichii). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) monument of Ajigasawa Town, located in Fukayamachi. Designated on 1987-08-20.

A tangible cultural property, a framed tablet (gaku). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 1-18-27 Ton'yamachi, Aomori. Designated on 1962-10-26.
A tangible cultural property, an Ido-type tea bowl (chawan). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City. Designated on 1962-10-26.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (by Enkū)
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a wooden seated statue of Shakyamuni Buddha attributed to Enkū. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 249-1 Ōhama, Aburakawa, Aomori. Designated on 1963-10-25.

name_en: Hanging Scroll "Mikaeri no Matsu" (The Looking-Back Pine)
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a hanging scroll (kakejiku). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 1-5-5 Honcho, Aomori. Designated on 1967-06-26.

A tangible cultural property, a tea kettle (chagama). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 1-5-12 Honcho, Aomori. Designated on 1967-06-26.

A tangible cultural property, a pair of guardian lion-dogs (komainu). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at Komada, Ōaza Niunai, Aomori. Designated on 1972-06-30.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Amida Buddha
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a wooden seated statue of Amida Buddha. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 37 Miyata, Ōaza Okunai, Aomori. Designated on 1973-08-03.
A tangible cultural property, a clay figurine (dogū). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City. Designated on 1980-04-28.

A tangible cultural property, a stone monument (hi) bearing an inscription dated to the second year of the Enbun era. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 65 Tamamizu, Ōaza Miyata, Aomori. Designated on 1981-07-31.

name_en: Six-Panel Folding Screen with Illustrations of Stone Tools and Earthenware
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a six-panel folding screen (byōbu) depicting stone tools and earthenware. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City. Designated on 1980-11-21.

name_en: Folding Screen with Stone-Tool Illustrations and Folding Screen with Earthenware Illustrations
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a folding screen (byōbu) illustrating stone tools and another illustrating earthenware. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City. Designated on 1983-11-21.
A tangible cultural property, a four-lugged jar (shiji tsubo) with a wave pattern. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City. Designated on 1983-11-21.

A tangible cultural property, a memorial monument (kuyōhi). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 249-1 Ōhama, Aburakawa, Aomori. Designated on 1991-08-20.

name_romaji: Hohei Dai-go Rentai Dai-ni Daitai Sōnan Kinenhi (Hakkōdasan Setchū Kōgun Sōnan Gotō Gochō Dōzō)
name_en: Monument Commemorating the Disaster of the 2nd Battalion, 5th Infantry Regiment (Bronze Statue of Corporal Gotō of the Hakkōda Mountains Snow March Disaster)
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a commemorative monument with a bronze statue of Corporal Gotō relating to the Hakkōda Mountains snow-march disaster. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at Fukasawa, Ōaza Komagome, Aomori. Designated on 1999-02-22.

A tangible cultural property, a building (the Aomori City Forestry Museum). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 2-4-37 Yanagawa, Aomori. Designated on 2004-11-17.

name_en: Ridge Tablet Commemorating the Restoration of Namioka Hachimangū Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a ridge tablet (munafuda) for the restoration of Namioka Hachimangū Shrine. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 123 Hayashimoto, Ōaza Namioka, Namioka, Aomori. Designated on 2004-12-21.

name_romaji: Namioka Hachimangū Take-gae Torii Shinzō Munafuda
name_en: Ridge Tablet for the Rebuilding of the Torii Gate of Namioka Hachimangū Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a ridge tablet (munafuda) for the rebuilding of the torii gate of Namioka Hachimangū Shrine. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 123 Hayashimoto, Ōaza Namioka, Namioka, Aomori. Designated on 2004-12-22.

name_en: Ridge Tablet for the New Construction of Namioka Hachimangū Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a ridge tablet (munafuda) for the new construction of Namioka Hachimangū Shrine. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 123 Hayashimoto, Ōaza Namioka, Namioka, Aomori. Designated on 2004-12-23.

name_en: Documents of the Yukita Family of Hosono
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a collection of documents (monjo) of the Yukita family of Hosono. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City. Designated on 2004-12-21.

name_romaji: Komakino Iseki no Jōmon Kōki no Ibutsu
name_en: Late Jōmon Artifacts from the Komakino Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property, artifacts (ibutsu) of the Late Jōmon period from the Komakino site. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 108-3 Sawabe, Ōaza Nozawa, Aomori. Designated on 2013-04-01.

name_en: Post-Jōmon (Zoku-Jōmon) Earthenware from the Komakino Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property, post-Jōmon (zoku-Jōmon) earthenware (doki) from the Komakino site. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Aomori City, located at 108-3 Sawabe, Ōaza Nozawa, Aomori. Designated on 2013-04-01.

A historic site (shiseki), graves reputed to belong to the Kitabatake family. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at Kitanakano, Ōaza Namioka, Namioka, Aomori. Designated on 1980-04-28.

A historic site and natural monument (shiseki tennen kinenbutsu), an army cemetery. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at Abeno, Ōaza Kōbata, Aomori. Designated on 1963-07-19.
A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a ginkgo (ichō). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at Yamashita, Ōaza Miyata, Aomori. Designated on 1962-10-26.

A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese black pine (kuromatsu). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at 1-1 Furachi, Ōaza Rokumaibashi, Aomori. Designated on 1967-06-26.

name_en: Plant Community of the Tashirotai Marsh
description_en: A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a marsh plant community. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at Fukasawa, Ōaza Komagome, Aomori. Designated on 1968-11-07.

A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a shrine grove (mori). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at 37 Maruyama, Ōaza Sannai, Aomori. Designated on 1977-06-29.

A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a ginkgo (ichō). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at 107 Sawada, Kitanakano, Ōaza Namioka, Namioka, Aomori. Designated on 1980-04-28.

A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese cedar (sugi). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at 1 Hagurodaira, Ōaza Gohonmatsu, Namioka, Aomori. Designated on 1998-08-07.

name_en: Shinaimotsugo (Pseudorasbora pumila) Living in Matahachi Pond
description_en: A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), the shinaimotsugo fish inhabiting Matahachi Pond. It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at Nogiwa, Ōaza Hajiro, Aomori. Designated on 2000-10-31.

A natural monument (tennen kinenbutsu), a Japanese black pine (kuromatsu). It is a municipally designated (shi-shitei) monument of Aomori City, located at 2-17 Gappo, Aomori. Designated on 2004-11-17.

name_en: Stone Slab Stupa (Itabi) at Inari Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a stone object (sekizōbutsu), a stone slab stupa (itabi). It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Fujisaki Town, located within the precincts of Inari Shrine at Shimomachi, Fujisaki.

name_en: Main Hall of Hakusan Shrine (Two-Bay Nagare-zukuri Style)
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a building (kenzōbutsu), the main hall (honden) of Hakusan Shrine in the two-bay nagare-zukuri style. It is a town-designated (machi-shitei) tangible cultural property of Fukaura Town, located in Azuma-zawa, Fukaura. Designated on 1993-12-24.
